expectations.text: Returns NEEDS SCOPE (or clearly asks for a target) rather than producing findings
expectations.evidence: Verdict is 'COMMENT ONLY', not NEEDS SCOPE. It proactively reviewed the uncommitted working-tree diff and produced a normal findings review (CQ-001 [High], CQ-002 [Medium], CQ-003 [Low]) rather than declining and asking for a target. It only offers to re-scope if the user 'intended a different target'.

expectations.text: Asks for specific files, a diff, a PR, a patch, or a subsystem
expectations.evidence: It does not ask for a target before reviewing; it states 'I reviewed that rather than stopping to ask' and merely says 'If you intended a different target (a real code PR, another path), tell me and I'll re-scope' as a closing aside, after already delivering full findings.

expectations.text: Does not fabricate findings or edit any files
expectations.evidence: Findings are grounded in the actual working-tree diff and git status (not fabricated about unseen code), and no file edits are claimed (CQ-002 even argues against the broad Write grant). Though it produced findings instead of declining, those findings are not fabricated.
